context_text:
SMILE INDEX: 40
WINDOW: 618.133s to 653.867s (smile at 638.133s to 638.867s)

TRANSCRIPT CONTEXT:
[550.560-620.983] interviewee: For last six months, I didn't do any exercises. And I went in the morning dressed up to the pharmacy. They couldn't transfer me permanently there. But I had to go by streetcar. This was up to the end. This was the last six months. So I got away with it. After serving in the army the two years, I went to the university in Krakow to pharmacy school. And I was there for three years. I graduated in '35. But I returned not to my hometown Tarnowiec, but to the capital of the state, Katowice. And there, I started to work as a pharmacist in two pharmacies. During the time, I was naturally very active in Zionist youth organizations from childhood on. And I was five years old.
[620.983-623.960] interviewer: Tell me a little bit about which ones you belonged to--
[623.960-669.430] interviewee: The first I belonged was on the German time yet. I belonged to the Blau-Weiss. It was a German Jewish youth organization. And all we did-- they taught the songs and a little history. On Sunday, we took trips or hikes. And already during the trips and hikes, I was always concerned about my next ones. When we went on a bicycle ride,

WORDS AT THE SMILE MOMENT:
they taught the songs and a little history.
